title: phlox pilosa subsp. ozarkana
intro: Phlox pilosa subsp. ozarkana, a subspecies of the fragrant phlox, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to the Ozark Mountains in the United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Phlox pilosa subsp. ozarkana include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
